mnemonics.title: Rank + Nullity = Columns
mnemonics.content: RANK + NULLITY = n (number of columns). Remember: 'Rank Never Underlies Loss In Y' - Rank + Nullity = dimension of domain.

mnemonics.title: Four Spaces Picture
mnemonics.content: Draw a rectangle divided into two: left side ℝⁿ has row space (top) and null space (bottom) perpendicular. Right side ℝᵐ has column space (top) and left null space (bottom) perpendicular.

mnemonics.title: Kernel + Image = Domain
mnemonics.content: KERNEL (null space) + IMAGE (range) = DOMAIN. Think: What goes in + What comes out = Total input space.

acronyms.usage: For any m×n matrix, remember that Rank + Nullity always equals the number of columns (n).

acronyms.expansion: Full rank = Onto, Into, Alone, Lie
acronyms.usage: Full row rank → Onto; Full column rank → Into (one-to-one); Full rank → Linearly independent rows/columns exist.

visualTips.visualization: Think of T as a machine: rank = how many independent output directions the machine can produce, nullity = how many input directions get crushed to zero.
Null space is the 'crusher' - all vectors in null space get mapped to zero. In 3D, if nullity=1, there's a line through origin that gets flattened to origin.

Draw two rectangles: left rectangle (ℝⁿ) has row space and null space as complementary orthogonal subspaces; right rectangle (ℝᵐ) has column space and left null space as complementary orthogonal subspaces.

analogies.analogy: Rank is like the number of 'useful independent directions' in a matrix - like having 3D coordinates but all points lie on a 2D plane means rank = 2.
Nullity represents 'redundancy' or 'degrees of freedom' in inputs that don't affect output - like multiple ways to press the same button.
Rank-Nullity is like conservation of energy: input dimension (V) is split into useful output (range) and wasted input (null), totaling original.
